acceptance_criteria: A finite graph model records vertex gauge choices, edge link phases, oriented paths, and closed loops.
Python examples compute path holonomy, path reversal, path concatenation, and plaquette loops.
Lean theorem targets are limited to finite path algebra and gauge-invariance facts.
The Living Book labels diagrams and widgets as explanations unless corresponding theorem ids are proved.
targets.guardrail: A finite gauge-link seed is not continuum electromagnetism, QED, Yang-Mills theory, or a physics prediction.
targets.next_action: Finite link-path record holonomy plus singleton, two-link, singleton-concat, three-link, four-link, empty-identity, concat-associativity, reversal-algebra, general boundary-checked append composability, first nonempty source/target laws, a checked finite path interface with explicit endpoints, identity laws, associative boundary-checked composition, checked-path holonomy laws, checked-to-link-path projection bridges, and closed checked-path gauge-invariance facts are now Lean-proved; next decide whether a full finite graph path category is worth adding.
targets.ordinary_baseline: Standard lattice gauge notation with link variables, Wilson loops, and separate prose or textbook derivations.
targets.question: Can Circle Calculus turn finite gauge-link diagrams into proof-carrying path holonomy and closed-loop invariance artifacts?
targets.title: Finite U(1)/Z_n lattice gauge Wilson-loop seed

acceptance_criteria: The paper clearly separates standard physics background from Circle Calculus interface claims.
Existing Hopf theorem ids are linked without claiming a full topological fibration proof.
At least one finite holonomy toy example is executable before any continuum claim is discussed.
The Living Book warning explains that Python examples and diagrams are not physics proofs.
The S3 and Physics Living Book pages expose a bounded Hopf hidden-phase widget linked to Hopf theorem and dictionary ids.
targets.guardrail: Do not claim Circle Calculus proves Berry phase, Aharonov-Bohm physics, or the full Hopf fibration before those objects are modeled.
targets.next_action: The bounded Hopf hidden-phase widget and Python parity check are in place; next draft a scoped holonomy primer that starts from finite loop phase and Hopf coordinate invariance before pointing to Berry/Aharonov-Bohm background.
targets.ordinary_baseline: Separate treatments of Berry phase, fiber bundles, Bloch sphere, and Hopf fibration.
targets.question: Can the existing Hopf phase-fiber spine become a proof-status-safe reader path for geometric phase, hidden phase, and holonomy?
targets.title: Berry/Hopf holonomy primer

acceptance_criteria: The next theorem targets model pure-imaginary vectors, norm preservation, and action composition only when the formal vector model is ready.
The paper keeps q/-q representative equality distinct from a completed SO(3) quotient.
Python orientation records compare raw representative state with action-equivalent state.
The Living Book exposes the q/-q sign ambiguity as a bounded conjugation-action widget with theorem-status badges and SO(3) guardrails.
targets.guardrail: Do not claim a complete Spin(3), SO(3), spinor, or quantum-spin formalization from the current sign-cancellation seed.
targets.next_action: The first q/-q Living Book widget and Python parity check are in place; next audit the S3 spin sidecar for pure-imaginary vector preservation, norm preservation, or action-composition theorems that improve orientation physics without requiring full SO(3).
targets.ordinary_baseline: Quaternion rotation formulas, rotation matrices, and separate sign-convention debugging tools.
targets.question: Can the current q/-q sign-cancellation proof grow into a verified spin/orientation lesson without overclaiming SO(3)?
targets.title: Quaternion spin quotient extension

acceptance_criteria: Finite periodic-dynamics examples record phase, winding, residue, period, and closure status.
Vortex-style examples are restricted to finite loop/winding toys before any continuum defect language.
Ordinary periodogram/Fourier or direct simulation baselines are named where benchmarking is involved.
targets.guardrail: Do not treat finite winding examples as proofs of continuum vortices, Kosterlitz-Thouless physics, or real material behavior.
targets.next_action: Add finite examples for stroboscopic phase, lifted winding count, and closed-loop winding around a marked defect.
targets.ordinary_baseline: Standard Floquet, action-angle, and topological-defect notation with separate diagrams and numerical examples.
targets.question: Can winding, residue, period, and closure make periodic dynamics and vortex-style winding examples easier to audit?
targets.title: Floquet, action-angle, and winding-defect finite fixtures

acceptance_criteria: A generator record stores seed, rule set, iteration schedule, closure condition, generated object, theorem ids, and dictionary ids.
Python examples regenerate finite circles, finite-circle diagrams, finite physics-loop diagrams, coils, orbit decompositions, or glyph fixtures exactly from generator records.
Lean targets prove finite generation/equality/closure facts where tractable.
The paper states the minimization criterion before using the word minimal.
targets.guardrail: A smaller generator is not automatically optimal, meaningful, or new mathematics.
targets.next_action: Exact representative-indexed orbit-decomposition regeneration, comparison gates, bounded finite-list generator search, generated-diagram widgets, and a proof-glyph certificate widget are in place; next extend the widget contract toward orbit-family records while keeping minimality claims scoped to explicit search spaces.
targets.ordinary_baseline: Value-only objects, finished diagrams, ordinary compressed files, or informal procedural descriptions.
targets.question: Can Circle Calculus represent an object by a small seed plus checked rules that regenerate the object, with proof status and provenance attached?
targets.title: Minimal generator provenance

acceptance_criteria: A deterministic benchmark compares explicit description length with seed-plus-rule description length.
The fixture checks exact regeneration before reporting any compactness or explanation result.
Negative cases are included where a generator is longer, less clear, or too contrived.
The benchmark reports proof coverage separately from byte/string length.
targets.guardrail: Do not call this universal compression, Kolmogorov complexity, or an optimal generator theorem.
targets.next_action: Finite-circle and finite physics-loop diagram generation are now in both the comparison fixture and Living Book widget; exact-regeneration self/symmetry/transitivity, field-equality gates, and unequal-value negative gates are Lean-proved; bounded_generator_search now ranks only declared finite candidate lists with exact and shorter-candidate counts; expand next toward orbit-family diagrams and stronger explicit search-space baselines.
targets.ordinary_baseline: Explicitly listing every node, edge, orbit, generated point, or diagram element.
targets.question: Can generator records give better explanation or reconstruction discipline than object-only descriptions on bounded Circle Calculus artifacts?
targets.title: Seed-plus-rules versus object-only description benchmark

acceptance_criteria: A generated diagram exposes its seed, rules, theorem ids, dictionary ids, and proof-status badges.
The site distinguishes generated explanation from formal proof.
At least one S1 generator diagram and one physics/gauge-loop placeholder use the same component contract.
targets.area: Generative diagrams and Living Book lessons
targets.guardrail: Generated diagrams are explanations and interfaces; they are not proofs unless a linked theorem id is independently Lean-proved.
targets.next_action: The Living Book now exposes finite-circle, finite physics-loop, and proof-glyph seed/rule records with theorem-status badges; next add a dedicated orbit-family widget or make more generator widgets data-driven from generated site fixtures.
targets.ordinary_baseline: Hand-authored static diagrams with separate source code and theorem references.
targets.question: Can a Living Book diagram be generated from the same seed/rule/provenance record that feeds Python examples and theorem ids?
targets.title: Proof-carrying generative diagrams
